place: Morris Plains borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The first settlers of Morris Plains were the Lenni Lenape, a Native American tribe of the Algonquin.
In 1848 the railroad was extended from Morristown through Morris Plains, sparking rapid growth.
Morris Plains became an incorporated borough on January 15 1926, electing a mayor and council that included one woman and five men.
Thomas Pierson arrived from England in 1685 and built a sawmill near Central Avenue on what is now Thompson Pond.
Construction of the State Hospital at Greystone Park began in 1874, marking a major public health project in the area.
The George Washington Coffee Company opened its doors in Morris Plains in 1927, adding to the borough’s early industrial mix.
